# Sourcemapper

High-performance tool to extract original JavaScript source code from sourcemaps generated by webpack, Vite, or similar bundlers. Features concurrent file writing, auto-detection, and batch processing capabilities.

An article explaining its purpose: [https://pulsesecurity.co.nz/articles/javascript-from-sourcemaps](https://pulsesecurity.co.nz/articles/javascript-from-sourcemaps)

## Features

- ✅ **Auto-detection** - Automatically detects `.map` (sourcemap) or `.js` (JavaScript) URLs
- ✅ **Batch processing** - Process multiple URLs from command line or file
- ✅ **Concurrent file writing** - Parallel extraction with worker pool
- ✅ **Retryable HTTP** - Auto-retry with exponential backoff via ProjectDiscovery's retryablehttp-go
- ✅ **Rate limiting** - Control request rate to avoid overwhelming targets
- ✅ **Proxy support** - Route traffic through HTTP/SOCKS5 proxies
- ✅ **Custom headers** - Add authentication, cookies, or any HTTP headers
- ✅ **Graceful error handling** - Continue processing on failures
- ✅ **Path sanitization** - Handle invalid characters in file paths

## Installation

### From Source

```bash
git clone https://github.com/anhnmt/sourcemapper
cd sourcemapper
go mod tidy
go build -o sourcemapper main.go
```

### Using Go Install

```bash
go install github.com/anhnmt/sourcemapper@latest
```

## Usage

```bash
sourcemapper [flags]

Flags:
  INPUT:
    -u, -url string[]     URL/path (auto-detects .map or .js, comma-separated)
    -l, -list string      File containing URLs (auto-detects .map or .js)

  OUTPUT:
    -o, -output string    Output directory (required)

  CONFIGURATION:
    -p, -proxy string     Proxy URL (http/socks5)
    -t, -timeout int      Request timeout in seconds (default 30)
    -r, -retries int      Number of retries (default 3)
    -rl, -rate-limit int  Requests per second (0 = unlimited)
    -c, -concurrency int  Concurrent file writers (default 5)
    -k, -insecure         Skip TLS verification (default true)
    -H, -header string[]  HTTP header (comma-separated)

  DEBUG:
    -s, -silent           Silent mode (errors only)
    -v, -verbose          Verbose mode
```

## Examples

### Basic Usage

```bash
# Single sourcemap URL
sourcemapper -o ./output -u https://example.com/app.js.map

# Single JavaScript URL (auto-finds sourcemap)
sourcemapper -o ./output -u https://example.com/bundle.js

# Multiple URLs (auto-detects types)
sourcemapper -o ./output -u app.js.map,bundle.js,vendor.js
```

### Batch Processing

```bash
# From file
sourcemapper -o ./output -l urls.txt

# Mix both
sourcemapper -o ./output -u app.js -l urls.txt
```

**urls.txt format:**
```
# Comments start with #
https://example.com/app.js.map
https://example.com/bundle.js
https://cdn.example.com/chunk.js?v=1.0
./local/vendor.js.map
```

### With Authentication

```bash
sourcemapper -o ./output \
  -u https://app.example.com/bundle.js \
  -H "Cookie: session=abc123" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer token"
```

### Through Proxy (Burp Suite)

```bash
sourcemapper -o ./output \
  -u https://target.com/app.js \
  -p http://127.0.0.1:8080 \
  -k
```

### Rate Limited (Stealth)

```bash
sourcemapper -o ./output \
  -u app.js,vendor.js \
  -rl 2 \
  -t 60 \
  -r 5
```

### Advanced Options

```bash
# Custom timeout, retries, and workers
sourcemapper -o ./output \
  -u bundle.js \
  -t 120 \
  -r 10 \
  -c 10 \
  -v
```

## Auto-Detection

URLs are automatically categorized based on file extension:

| Pattern | Type | Action |
|---------|------|--------|
| `*.map` | Sourcemap | Direct extraction |
| `*.js` | JavaScript | Find and extract sourcemap |
| `*.map?*` | Sourcemap with query | Direct extraction |
| `*.js?*` | JavaScript with query | Find and extract sourcemap |

**Examples:**
- `app.js.map` → Sourcemap
- `bundle.js` → JavaScript (auto-finds sourcemap)
- `chunk.js?v=1.0.0` → JavaScript
- `app.js.map?hash=abc` → Sourcemap

## Output Structure

```
output/
├── webpack/_N_E/
│   ├── node_modules/
│   │   └── next/dist/...
│   └── webpack/runtime/
│       └── ...
└── src/
    ├── components/
    └── utils/
```

All sources are extracted to a single output directory with preserved directory structure.

## URL List File Format

Create a file with one URL per line:

```txt
# Example urls.txt
# Lines starting with # are comments

# Production URLs
https://app.example.com/static/main.js
https://app.example.com/static/vendor.js.map

# CDN URLs
https://cdn.example.com/bundle.js?v=2.0.0

# Local files
./downloads/app.js.map
/tmp/maps/chunk-123.js.map
```

Run with:
```bash
sourcemapper -o ./extracted -l urls.txt
```

## Performance

**Concurrent File Writing:**
- Uses worker pool (default: 5 workers)
- Parallel directory creation
- Non-blocking I/O operations

**Optimized HTTP:**
- Connection pooling (100 max idle connections)
- Auto-retry with exponential backoff
- Configurable timeouts and retries

**Example Output:**
```
[+] Auto-detected: 5 sourcemap URLs, 3 JavaScript URLs
[+] HTTP Client configured: timeout=30s, retries=3, insecure=true

[*] Processing sourcemap 1/5: https://example.com/app.js.map
[+] Retrieved Sourcemap with version 3, containing 125 entries
[+] Writing 4262 bytes to output/webpack:/app/components/Button.js
...
[+] Successfully processed 125 out of 125 source entries

[*] Processing JavaScript 1/3: https://example.com/bundle.js
[.] Found SourceMap in JavaScript body: bundle.js.map
[+] Retrieved Sourcemap with version 3, containing 89 entries
...

============================================================
[+] SUMMARY: Processed 8 sourcemaps successfully, 0 failed
[+] Total source files extracted: 543
[+] Done
```

## Use Cases

### Web Application Reconnaissance

```bash
# Extract all Next.js sources
sourcemapper -o ./recon \
  -u https://target.com/_next/static/chunks/main.js \
  -u https://target.com/_next/static/chunks/pages/_app.js \
  -u https://target.com/_next/static/chunks/framework.js
```

### Bug Bounty Research

```bash
# Slow and steady
sourcemapper -o ./research \
  -l targets.txt \
  -rl 2 \
  -t 90 \
  -r 5
```

### Pentesting

```bash
# Through Burp with authentication
sourcemapper -o ./pentest \
  -u https://target.com/app.js \
  -p http://127.0.0.1:8080 \
  -H "Cookie: auth=xyz" \
  -k \
  -v
```

### CI/CD Integration

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Extract and audit production sources
DATE=$(date +%Y%m%d)
sourcemapper -o ./audit-$DATE \
  -u https://prod.example.com/main.js \
  -t 60 \
  -r 3

# Scan for secrets
grep -r "API_KEY\|PASSWORD\|SECRET" ./audit-$DATE/
```

## Security Notes

⚠️ **Malicious JavaScript Warning**: sourcemapper will retrieve any URL referenced as a sourcemap. A malicious JavaScript file can force sourcemapper to make requests to arbitrary URLs.

**Best Practices:**
- Use `-insecure` only in controlled environments
- Be mindful of rate limits with `-rl` flag
- Use proxy (`-p`) when testing production sites
- Check extracted code for sensitive information before sharing

## Error Handling

**Graceful Degradation:**
- Continues processing remaining URLs on failure
- Handles mismatched `sources` and `sourcesContent` arrays
- Sanitizes invalid path characters (`|`, `:`, `?`, `*`, etc.)
- Creates parent directories automatically

**Example:**
```
[!] WARNING: sourcesContent array (16 entries) is shorter than sources array (20 entries)
[!] Only processing the first 16 entries that have content available
[+] Successfully processed 16 out of 20 source entries
```

## Dependencies

- Go 1.21+
- [github.com/projectdiscovery/goflags](https://github.com/projectdiscovery/goflags) - CLI framework
- [github.com/projectdiscovery/retryablehttp-go](https://github.com/projectdiscovery/retryablehttp-go) - Retryable HTTP client

## Supported SourceMap Specifications

- [Source Map Revision 3](https://tc39.es/source-map-spec/)
- Inline sourcemaps (`data:` URIs)
- `SourceMap` and `X-SourceMap` (deprecated) headers
- `//# sourceMappingURL=` and `//@` comments
